messages.content: I've an asp.net core mvc project, need you to create an "add" view page that get "product" class data from the user, it contains name, text, image. use productviewmodel to get the data from the view. and use Byte[] datatype for img saving. design the form in the viewpage using tailwindcss. and save the data in the product table in the applicationdbcontext using the best, fastest practises.
Made the changes, still not working.
write a code to store the data to the controller in a different way than AdsViewModel.
how to display back an image saved that way?
do it all inside the view.
The view page is a list of the db records for Ads, write it again to work inside the foreach of the list. Also mention what namespaces do i need to use for GetImageData() ?
error: the name “GetImageData” does not exist in the current context
It must be all done within the view.
edit this: "" to show both JPEG and PNG
just make an if statement to check for the image type weither it's JPEG or PNG. and edit this "" accordingly.
write a code in the controller so that the viewpage returns only for the user "MohamedElazab249@gmail.com"
Make it all happen within the controller. i don't have startup.cs
How to publish asp.net core mvc project from local folder up to Windows Server IIS, and what to do about the dbcontext

To deploy an ASP.NET Core MVC project to IIS on a Windows Server, follow these steps:

1. Install the .NET Core Hosting Bundle on the server:

   1. Download the .NET Core Hosting Bundle installer from this link: https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/thank-you/runtime-aspnetcore-6.0.2-windows-hosting-bundle-installer (Make sure to download the appropriate version of the hosting bundle for your .NET Core MVC project).
   2. Run the installer on your server to install the .NET Core runtime, libraries, and the ASP.NET Core Module for IIS.
   3. After the installation is finished, restart the IIS to load the new ASP.NET Core Module.

2. Publish the project:

   1. Open Visual Studio, right-click on your project, and then click "Publish".
   2. Choose the folder option, create a new folder profile or select an existing one, then click "Publish" to build and publish your project to the specified folder. You can also use the `dotnet publish` command to publish your project from the command line.

3. Configure the connection string for the `DbContext`:

   1. Open the `appsettings.json` file in your published folder.
   2. Change the connection string to match your production database settings.
   3. Save the modified `appsettings.json` file.

4. Migrate the database schema:

   1. Open a command prompt.
   2. Change the directory to the folder containing the published application.
   3. Run the following command to migrate the schema to the production database: `dotnet YourProject.dll
